title: Print Hello World
description: Write a program that prints 'Hello, World!' to the screen.
example:
Output:
Hello, World!
solution: print("Hello, World!")

title: Greet By Name
description: Write a program that asks the user for their name and prints a greeting.
example:
Input: Enter your name: Alice
Output:
Hello, Alice!
solution:
name = input("Enter your name: ")
print("Hello, " + name + "!")

title: Add Two Numbers
description: Ask the user for two numbers, add them, and print the result.
example:
Input: Enter first number: 3
Enter second number: 5
Output:
8
solution:
a = float(input("Enter first number: "))
b = float(input("Enter second number: "))
print("Output:", a + b)

title: Check Positive/Negative
description: Ask the user for a number, then print whether it is positive, negative, or zero.
example:
Input: Enter a number: -3
Output:
The number is negative.

title: Age Until 100
description: Ask the user for their age and tell them how many years until they turn 100.
example:
Input: Enter your age: 20
Output:
You will turn 100 in 80 years.
solution:
age = int(input("Enter your age: "))
years = 100 - age
print("Output: You will turn 100 in", years, "years.")

title: Celsius to Fahrenheit
description: Convert a temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit. Ask the user for a temperature in Celsius.
example:
Input: Enter temperature in Celsius: 0
Output:
32°F
solution:
celsius = float(input("Enter temperature in Celsius: "))
fahrenheit = (celsius * 9/5) + 32
print("Output:", fahrenheit, "°F")

title: Rectangle Perimeter
description: Ask the user for the width and height of a rectangle and print its perimeter.
example:
Input: Enter width: 5
Enter height: 3
Output:
Perimeter is 16.
solution:
width = float(input("Enter width: "))
height = float(input("Enter height: "))
perimeter = 2 * (width + height)
print("Output: Perimeter is", perimeter)

title: Even or Odd
description: Ask the user for a number and print whether it is even or odd.
example:
Input: Enter a number: 4
Output:
The number is even.

title: Times Table
description: Ask the user for a number and print its multiplication table from 1 to 10.
example:
Input: Enter a number: 3
Output:
3 x 1 = 3
3 x 2 = 6
... 
3 x 10 = 30

title: Count from 1 to 10
description: Use a loop to print numbers from 1 to 10.
solution:
for i in range(1, 11):
    print(i)

title: Calculate Total Price
description: Ask the user for price per item and quantity, then calculate the total price.
example:
Input: Enter price: 10
Enter quantity: 3
Output:
Total is 30.
solution:
price = float(input("Enter price: "))
quantity = int(input("Enter quantity: "))
total = price * quantity
print("Output: Total is", total)

title: Area of a Triangle
description: Ask the user for the base and height of a triangle, then calculate its area.
example:
Input: Enter base: 5
Enter height: 4
Output:
Area is 10.
solution:
base = float(input("Enter base: "))
height = float(input("Enter height: "))
area = 0.5 * base * height
print("Output: Area is", area)

title: Count Up to N
description: Ask the user for a number N and print all numbers from 1 to N.
example:
Input: Enter N: 5
Output:
1, 2, 3, 4, 5

title: Word Length
description: Ask the user for a word and print the number of characters in it.
example:
Input: Enter a word: hello
Output:
Length is 5.
solution:
word = input("Enter a word: ")
print("Output: Length is", len(word))

title: Reverse a Word
description: Ask the user for a word and print the word in reverse order.
example:
Input: Enter a word: Python
Output:
nohtyP
solution:
word = input("Enter a word: ")
print("Output:", word[::-1])

title: Convert Hours to Minutes
description: Ask the user for a number of hours and convert it to minutes.
example:
Input: Enter hours: 2
Output:
120 minutes.
solution:
hours = float(input("Enter hours: "))
minutes = hours * 60
print("Output:", minutes, "minutes.")

title: Convert Kilometers to Meters
description: Ask the user for a distance in kilometers and convert it to meters.
example:
Input: Enter kilometers: 3
Output:
3000 meters.
solution:
km = float(input("Enter kilometers: "))
meters = km * 1000
print("Output:", meters, "meters.")

title: Print Larger Number
description: Ask the user for two numbers and print the larger one.
example:
Input: Enter first number: 7
Enter second number: 5
Output:
7 is larger.

title: Count Words in a Sentence
description: Ask the user for a sentence and print the number of words in it.
example:
Input: Enter sentence: Hello world
Output:
2 words.
solution:
sentence = input("Enter sentence: ")
words = sentence.split()
print("Output:", len(words), "words.")

title: Square a Number
description: Ask the user for a number and print its square.
example:
Input: Enter number: 4
Output:
Square is 16.
solution:
num = float(input("Enter number: "))
print("Output: Square is", num ** 2)

title: Print First 5 Multiples of 5
description: Print the first 5 multiples of 5.
solution:
for i in range(1, 6):
    print(5 * i, end=", " if i < 5 else "\n")

title: Area of a Circle
description: Ask the user for the radius of a circle and calculate its area (use pi = 3.14).
example:
Input: Enter radius: 3
Output:
Area is approximately 28.26.
solution:
radius = float(input("Enter radius: "))
area = 3.14 * radius * radius
print("Output: Area is approximately", area)

title: Average of Two Numbers
description: Ask the user for two numbers and print their average.
example:
Input: Enter number 1: 4
Enter number 2: 6
Output:
Average is 5.
solution:
a = float(input("Enter number 1: "))
b = float(input("Enter number 2: "))
avg = (a + b) / 2
print("Output: Average is", avg)

title: Approximate Days Old
description: Ask the user for their age in years and estimate how many days old they are (assume 365 days/year).
example:
Input: Enter age: 10
Output:
Approximately 3650 days.
solution:
age = int(input("Enter age: "))
days = age * 365
print("Output: Approximately", days, "days.")

title: Print ASCII Value
description: Ask the user for a single character and print its ASCII value.
example:
Input: Enter character: A
Output:
ASCII value is 65.
solution:
ch = input("Enter character: ")
print("Output: ASCII value is", ord(ch))
